m2m-handoff.load-bearing-primitive:
A locally-runnable, Terraform-generated fleet of Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects + KV + R2 + Queues + Workflows that accepts the exact request bodies and emits the exact SSE event shapes a real consumer of Anthropic's Managed Agents Beta API would consume — namely the endpoints under /v1/agents, /v1/sessions, /v1/environments, /v1/skills, /v1/memory_stores, /v1/files, /v1/vaults, /v1/webhooks, /v1/user_profiles, and /v1/messages — with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY rejected at every layer and /v1/messages driven by a content-addressed fixture-replay engine instead of upstream inference.
Collapse modes if removed or weakened: - INV-02 (Terraform-only authoring) loses its concrete target. - INV-03 (wire format = contract) collapses to vibes. - The chassis cannot dogfood managed-agent client code without a billed account or a real key. - The OAuth-only invariant gets re-litigated every PR.

directives.d: Author every Worker, Durable Object class, binding, route, KV namespace, R2 bucket, Queue, Workflow, and secret-reference (NOT secret value) inside HCL under `infra/terraform/managed-agents-mock/*.tf`. wrangler.toml fragments under `build/wrangler/*.toml` are GENERATED from these modules — never hand-written. Generation is wired into `terraform apply` via a `local_file` or `null_resource` provisioner; the generated files are also committed so CI can read them without a TF run.

Treat the managed-agents wire format as the contract. Worker request validators and SSE event emitters MUST match upstream byte-for-byte: header names, event type strings, JSON field names, RFC 3339 timestamps, error envelope shape. Conformance is asserted by replaying official Anthropic SDK clients (Python, TypeScript, Go, Ruby) against the Worker fleet under miniflare with `base_url` re-pointed at 127.0.0.1.

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be REJECTED, never accepted, at every entry point. Defense in depth: (a) Worker `sanitizeEnv()` reads `env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and throws if non-empty, before any binding is touched. (b) The Worker's auth middleware accepts the `x-api-key` HEADER (SDKs require it), but treats the value as an opaque tenant identifier. Any value matching the regex `^sk-ant-` returns HTTP 400 with body `{error:{type:"invalid_api_key_format", message:"see m2m DIR-03"}}`. (c) Terraform plan REFUSES to render any `cloudflare_workers_secret` resource whose `name` equals `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` via a `lifecycle.precondition`. (d) miniflare env sets the var to the literal string `"REJECTED-SEE-DIR-03"` so any code path that reads it fails loud, not silent.

`/v1/messages` (and any internal call from a Worker into the inference layer) is served by a content-addressed fixture-replay engine. Request fingerprint is `sha256(canonical_json({system,messages,tools,model}))`. Cache miss under miniflare MUST 404 with the precise hash so the operator can drop the missing fixture in; it MUST NOT silently call out to api.anthropic.com or any other inference endpoint.

Every commit ends with `(O)` where N matches the outcome it lands. Multiple outcomes per commit => `(O1,O3)`. The convention test (`src/lib/conventions.test.ts`) enforces this for commits after 2026-05-15T04:30Z.

Every test file ships an `@cite` header pointing at `vendor/`, `seeds/`, or `rubrics/`. Citations to upstream HTTPS URLs go in `` elements in this XML; in-tree code points at the local vendor mirror. Enforced by `scripts/lib/citation-guard.ts`.

invariants.i: OAuth-only. `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` is the only Anthropic credential the chassis ever holds. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is rejected at every layer.
Terraform is the only authoring surface. Worker source, DO classes, KV/R2/Queue/D1 bindings, routes, secrets-references (NOT values), and wrangler.toml derivatives are all generated from .tf modules.
The managed-agents wire format is the contract. Workers MUST accept the same request bodies and emit the same SSE event shapes a real consumer would expect.

Hermetic miniflare. The simulation runs under `npx miniflare` (or `@cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers`) with no outbound network. Any call from a Worker to platform.claude.com, api.anthropic.com, or any other inference endpoint MUST be intercepted and either (a) served from fixtures, or (b) error with `ENETUNREACH-by-design`.

Beta header pinning. Every Worker route requires `anthropic-beta: managed-agents-2026-04-01` (case-insensitive). Requests missing it return `400 missing_beta_header` in the upstream error envelope. The beta value lives in a single Terraform variable (`local.beta_header`) so a future bump (e.g., `managed-agents-2026-07-01`) flips every Worker and every fixture at once.

cite.what: Repo posture: OAuth-only, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY forbidden, managed-agents-api use='false'

m.to: DO class `AgentRegistry` (one DO per agent_id, append-only version log) + D1 row for indexed list/search
DO class `EnvironmentRecord` + KV namespace `env-metadata` for fast list; `EnvironmentBootstrap` Workflow seeds template (no real container under miniflare)
DO class `SessionRoom` (one DO per session_id) — holds event log, thread tree, idle/running state; archival writes a JSONL bundle to R2 bucket `sessions-archive`
Append-only log in `SessionRoom` storage; SSE delivery via an `sse-broker` Worker that tails the DO + a Queue for fan-out; mirrored to R2 for archival
DO class `SkillStore` + R2 bucket `skills-bundles` (one tarball per version); `SkillIngest` Workflow validates frontmatter and computes content hash
DO class `MemoryStore` — entries append-only; redaction emits a NEW MemoryVersion that masks ranges in the prior version (prior version retained)
DO class `VaultStore` — credentials enveloped with AES-GCM keyed by a Worker-bound master key (Cloudflare Secrets Store in prod via TF; deterministic stub under miniflare)
R2 bucket `files-content` (content-addressed by SHA256); D1 row for metadata (name, mime, size, uploader_tenant, uploaded_at)
DO class `WebhookSubs` + Queue `webhook-out`; consumer Worker signs payloads (HMAC-SHA256) and POSTs; miniflare sinks to a local fixture server at 127.0.0.1:8080
D1 table `user_profiles` + DO `UserEnrollment` for the one-time enrollment URL flow
`messages-worker` consults R2 fixtures (content-addressed). Cache miss => 404 with hash. Under miniflare it NEVER makes outbound calls.
Shared middleware `requireBetaHeader('managed-agents-2026-04-01')` injected into every Worker via a TF variable + module
Worker entrypoint runs `sanitizeEnv()` that throws if env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is non-empty; auth middleware rejects header values matching ^sk-ant-

outcome.title: Posture + invariant CI gate
outcome.acceptance: - `infra/terraform/managed-agents-mock/_invariants.tf` exists and exports `local.beta_header = "managed-agents-2026-04-01"` plus a `precondition` that fails plan if any variable name matches `/^ANTHROPIC_API_KEY$/`. - CI workflow `.github/workflows/managed-agents-mock-invariants.yml` greps every path under `infra/terraform/managed-agents-mock/**` for any new occurrence of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and fails the build if found. - `scripts/verify-managed-agents-mock.ts` runs locally and exits 0 on a clean checkout.

outcome.title: Wire-format contract pinned to vendor/ mirror
outcome.acceptance: - `scripts/extract-wire-types.ts` parses the Beta API .md files under `vendor/anthropics/platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/` and emits `src/wire/managed-agents/types.ts` with one exported type per `BetaManagedAgents*` schema name (Agent, AgentVersion, Session, SessionThread, EventUnion, SkillVersion, MemoryStore, MemoryVersion, Credential, File, Webhook, UserProfile, MessageCreateParams, MessageCreateResponse, ErrorEnvelope). - The extractor writes a manifest `infra/terraform/managed-agents-mock/wire-manifest.json` enumerating every endpoint path + method, consumed by OUT-03 to generate routes. - A round-trip test (`src/wire/managed-agents/types.spec.ts`) asserts each generated type can JSON-parse a fixture extracted from `vendor/.../api/beta//*`.
outcome.cites: [C-API-AGENTS, C-API-SESSIONS, C-API-ENVS, C-API-SKILLS, C-API-MEMORY, C-API-FILES, C-API-VAULTS, C-API-WEBHOOKS, C-API-USERS, C-API-MSG, C-API-MODELS]

outcome.title: Terraform modules for Cloudflare primitives
outcome.acceptance: - `infra/terraform/managed-agents-mock/modules/` contains one module per Worker: agents-worker, sessions-worker, environments-worker, skills-worker, memory-worker, files-worker, vaults-worker, webhooks-worker, users-worker, messages-worker, sse-broker. - Each module declares: `cloudflare_workers_script`, one `cloudflare_workers_durable_objects_namespace` per DO class it owns, KV/R2/Queue/D1 bindings, and a generated wrangler.toml under `build/wrangler/.toml` for miniflare. - `terraform plan` against a `local` backend produces a zero-diff plan against the committed snapshot under `infra/terraform/managed-agents-mock/.plan-snapshot/`. - `terraform validate` is wired into CI.
outcome.cites: [C-CF-TF, C-CF-DO, C-CF-WORKFLOWS, C-CF-QUEUES, C-CF-R2, C-CF-D1, C-CF-KV]

outcome.title: Miniflare bindings + per-test isolation fixtures
outcome.acceptance: - `scripts/miniflare-up.ts` constructs a single `new Miniflare({ workers: [...] })` loading each `build/wrangler/.toml` produced by OUT-03, wires shared KV / R2 / Queue / D1 namespaces by name, pins `compatibilityDate` and `compatibilityFlags` from a single TF-rendered fragment, and binds 127.0.0.1:8787. - Per-test isolation: a vitest helper resets every DO storage shard and KV/R2 namespace between tests, parametrised by `MF_TEST_ID`. - `tests/managed-agents-mock/fixtures/` pre-seeds: (a) one agent (claude-opus-4-7-based), (b) one environment template (`generic-2026-04`), (c) one skill bundle (.tar.gz) under R2, (d) one user_profile + enrollment URL fragment, (e) one webhook subscription pointing at 127.0.0.1:8080/sink. - Smoke test `npm run test:mock:smoke` starts miniflare, runs a session-create + first `user.message` + `agent.message` round-trip, and shuts down with `await mf.dispose()`.

outcome.title: SSE event shape parity (no inference required)
outcome.acceptance: - For every event type enumerated in `vendor/.../managed-agents/events-and-streaming.md` and every `BetaManagedAgents*Event` declared in `vendor/.../api/beta/sessions.md` (initial set: `agent.message`, `agent.thinking`, `agent.custom_tool_use`, `agent.subagent_*`, `user.message`, `user.custom_tool_result`, `session.state`, plus `BetaManagedAgentsSessionLifecycle*`), `tests/managed-agents-mock/events/.spec.ts` asserts: - emitted byte sequence matches `data: \n\n` framing, - all required fields present per the wire type, - `processed_at` is RFC 3339, - sub-session events carry `session_thread_id` when present. - A fuzzer `scripts/fuzz-events.ts` generates 500 random event orderings (within the lifecycle FSM) and asserts both the official Python and TypeScript SDK parsers accept every one without warnings.

outcome.title: Auth middleware: reject ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, accept opaque tenant id
outcome.acceptance: - Shared module `src/wire/managed-agents/auth.ts`: - reads `x-api-key` header, - rejects regex `^sk-ant-` with HTTP 400 and body `{error:{type:"invalid_api_key_format", message:"see m2m DIR-03"}}`, - otherwise treats the value as `tenant_id` and stamps it on every persisted row. - Worker `env-sanitizer` reads `env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and throws if non-empty. A test asserts boot-time failure when the var is set in `wrangler.toml`. - Terraform plan rejects any `cloudflare_workers_secret` resource whose `name` equals `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` via a `lifecycle.precondition`.

outcome.title: Messages fixture-replay engine
outcome.acceptance: - `messages-worker` computes `req_hash = sha256(canonical_json({system, messages, tools, model, extra_headers_filtered}))`. - On hit: streams the recorded response. Stream framing matches `anthropic-sdk-typescript`'s expected SSE shape: `message_start` -> `content_block_start` -> `content_block_delta`* -> `content_block_stop` -> `message_delta` -> `message_stop`. - On miss under miniflare: returns `404 {error:{type:"fixture_miss", hash:""}}` and writes a breadcrumb to `fixtures/messages/_missing/.json` capturing the request body so the operator can record the response. - 6 canned fixtures land under `fixtures/messages/.json`, covering: simple text reply, single tool_use, parallel tool_use, extended-thinking, refusal, message_batch chunk.

outcome.title: Vaults + credentials with local AES-GCM stub
outcome.acceptance: - `VaultStore` DO encrypts credential plaintexts with `crypto.subtle.encrypt({name:"AES-GCM"}, key, plaintext)`. The key is derived from a per-vault salt + a Worker-bound master key. In prod the master key is wired to Cloudflare Secrets Store via TF; under miniflare it's a deterministic stub set through `env.MOCK_VAULT_MASTER` (never echoed to logs). - `POST /v1/vaults/{id}/credentials` and the `mcp_oauth_validate` endpoint match the upstream `BetaManagedAgentsCredential*` schemas. - Round-trip test: create vault, add credential, retrieve (with redaction — plaintext never returned), validate (mocked OAuth bearer flow), archive, list, delete.

outcome.title: Skills ingest workflow
outcome.acceptance: - `SkillIngest` Cloudflare Workflow: (1) accepts tarball upload via R2 multipart, (2) extracts and validates `SKILL.md` frontmatter against the shape Anthropic's CLI ingests, (3) computes content hash, stores bundle at `skills-bundles//.tar.gz`, (4) writes a SkillVersion row to D1 with provenance. - Test loads a bundle synthesised from one of this repo's existing skills under `.claude/skills//SKILL.md` (heartbeat, routines, refresh-vendors, schedule-bridge) and asserts the workflow completes successfully.

outcome.title: Memory stores + redaction-as-new-version
outcome.acceptance: - `MemoryStore` DO append-only model: create memory, list memories, retrieve, update (writes a new MemoryVersion; previous version retained), delete (tombstone, not physical), archive (DO emits a final `memory_store.archived` event), redact (creates a new MemoryVersion masking specified ranges). - Test asserts that after redact, `list memory_versions` returns the redacted version PLUS the prior versions, and `retrieve memory_version ` still returns the un-redacted plaintext — i.e., redaction is on a NEW version, per upstream semantics.

outcome.title: Multi-agent / subagent thread tree
outcome.acceptance: - `SessionRoom` stores threads in a tree keyed by `session_thread_id`. Root thread id == `:root`. - `agent.subagent_*` events flow up to the parent thread's stream with `session_thread_id` set; the subagent's own thread also retains the event. - Conformance: replay the multi-agent example from `vendor/.../managed-agents/multi-agent.md` (coordinator + two children) and assert all event types arrive in BOTH the per-thread and root streams with matching `id`s.

outcome.title: Webhook delivery with signed payloads + retries
outcome.acceptance: - `WebhookSubs` DO holds subscriptions. Outbound delivery via Queue `webhook-out`; consumer Worker signs the body with HMAC-SHA256 keyed by the subscription's secret. Retry policy: 5 attempts with exponential backoff (2s, 8s, 30s, 2m, 10m). - Under miniflare, sink is a small HTTP fixture server at 127.0.0.1:8080 that records every delivery into `fixtures/webhooks/received/*.json`. - Test asserts: simulated 500 from sink triggers retry; simulated 200 records exactly one delivery; HMAC verifies against the stored secret.

outcome.title: Files API (upload, download, list, delete) on R2
outcome.acceptance: - Upload writes to R2 `files-content//`, dedup by content hash, returns a `file_id` to the caller. - Download supports HTTP Range requests; a large-file test reads a 200 MB synthetic file with `Range: bytes=N-M`. - File metadata persisted to D1 `files`: `id, name, mime, size, sha256, uploader_tenant, created_at`. - PDF + image stubs: vision/PDF metadata extraction is stubbed under miniflare (returns canned EXIF and pageCount rather than parsing). Stubs recorded under `fixtures/files/`.

outcome.title: Conformance harness: real SDK clients drive the mock
outcome.acceptance: - Under `tests/managed-agents-mock/sdk-conformance/`: - Python: `anthropic.Anthropic(base_url="http://127.0.0.1:8787", api_key="tenant-A")` - TypeScript: `@anthropic-ai/sdk` configured similarly, - Go: `anthropic-sdk-go` with `option.WithBaseURL`, - Ruby: `anthropic` gem with `base_uri`. Each suite is a top-level `describe` that creates an agent, environment, session, runs a 3-turn conversation with one `tool_use` round-trip, archives the session, and asserts the R2 archival JSONL matches the in-DO event log. - The harness sets `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=tenant-A` style values ONLY where the SDK requires the env var to bootstrap — never a real key. The Worker rejects any value matching `^sk-ant-`.
outcome.depends_on: [OUT-05, OUT-06, OUT-07, OUT-08, OUT-09, OUT-10, OUT-11, OUT-12, OUT-13]

outcome.title: @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers integration
outcome.acceptance: - `tests/managed-agents-mock/vitest.config.ts` uses `@cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers` so tests execute inside workerd via miniflare. - Unit tests can `import { env } from "cloudflare:test"` and drive DO stubs directly without HTTP. - The conformance suite from OUT-14 runs in BOTH modes: in-process (fast) via the pool, and out-of-process via `mf.dispatchFetch` (slower but byte-faithful).

outcome.title: Vendor mirroring refreshed weekly
outcome.acceptance: - `scripts/crawl-vendors.ts` includes: - `https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/*.md` (~18 pages), - `https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/**` (~50 resource pages), - `https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/testing/miniflare/**`, - top-level index pages for `developers.cloudflare.com/{durable-objects,r2,d1,queues,workflows,kv}/`. - `seeds/citations/managed-agents-cf-miniflare.md` records the SHA of each fetched page and is updated by the weekly `/schedule refresh-vendors` routine. - Conformance test: every `cite href` in THIS m2m doc resolves to a present file under `vendor/`.

outcome.title: Permission policies + outcome grading parity
outcome.acceptance: - `permission_policies` field is supported on both agent and session payloads. A policy denies any tool call whose `name` matches a deny pattern, or any URL fetched by `web_fetch` outside an allow list. - When a session is created with an `outcome`, a separate `grader-worker` evaluates the rubric per criterion using fixture replays from `fixtures/messages/grader/*`, emitting `outcome.evaluation.start/ongoing/end` events. Rubric format matches this chassis's existing `rubrics/phase-*.md` conventions (separate context window per criterion). - Test asserts that a tool call blocked by policy raises a `policy.deny` event AND surfaces a refusal-shaped `agent.message` containing the policy id.

outcome.title: MCP connector compatibility (service bindings + remote MCP)
outcome.acceptance: - Agent config field `mcp_servers` accepts both: (a) a public MCP server URL (e.g., `mcp.example.com/mcp`), (b) an in-process Worker-MCP via Cloudflare Service Bindings to a sibling Worker. - The session's tool router exposes MCP tools to the agent under `mcp____` per upstream convention. - Conformance: a tiny `echo-mcp-worker` Worker registered as a service binding answers `tools/list` and `tools/call`, and a session uses it via fixture-replay.

outcome.title: EnvironmentBootstrap as a Cloudflare Workflow
outcome.acceptance: - `EnvironmentBootstrap` Cloudflare Workflow runs the quickstart-equivalent steps: register packages, establish allowlist, write a metadata blob to KV `env-metadata`, mark the environment `ready`. - Under miniflare, "container" startup is simulated end-to-end in-process; no real container is launched. A flag `MOCK_CONTAINER_SUCCESS=1` (default) forces success; `MOCK_CONTAINER_SUCCESS=0` forces a representative failure event so we can test the upstream `environment.failed` shape.

outcome.title: Single-command lifecycle: `npm run mock:up` -> `:down`
outcome.acceptance: - `npm run mock:up` runs: (1) `terraform plan` against the local backend (zero-diff vs snapshot), (2) wrangler.toml generation from TF outputs, (3) miniflare boot, (4) seed fixtures, (5) health check on each `/v1/` route, (6) prints a ready banner with the local URL. - `npm run mock:down` disposes miniflare, snapshots the R2/KV/D1/DO state under `.mock-state/`, and exits. - CI runs `up` -> conformance suite -> `down`.

state.todo_propagation: Per the upstream rule carried from kg.claude-mcp-browser-md v0.2 (operator instruction: "todos remain open"), every outcome here starts open. None are claimed in this handoff.
state.pr: draft (to be opened by the writing session)

next-session-instructions.step: Read `seeds/posture/session-start.xml` first to load auth posture and OAuth-only invariants.
Read this handoff (`seeds/handoffs/m2m-2026-05-18-managed-agents-cf-miniflare.xml`) to load the load-bearing primitive, directives, invariants, and open outcomes.
Pick the next open outcome (default: OUT-01) and execute under the chassis's commit-per-outcome discipline (`(O)` suffix).
When you find a fork or alternative approach, APPEND a new `` rather than rewriting an existing one. Use `` if the new outcome replaces an older one.
If `vendor/anthropics/platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/beta/.md` lacks a schema you need, refresh the vendor mirror (`npm run crawl:vendor -- anthropics`) before proposing wire-format changes.
Never set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` anywhere — not in `.env`, not in `wrangler.toml`, not in GitHub Actions, not in Terraform variables. DIR-03 is load-bearing.
